target.title: Make setup wizard resilient to richer gateway protocol steps
target.body:
## Summary
- Make gateway setup wizard handling resilient to non-interactive progress/status steps and future action-style protocol steps.
- Share wizard step classification, auth URL/code formatting, and auth-aware timeout logic between headless setup and the WinUI wizard page.
- Preserve raw option values for unknown choice-style steps and harden gateway restart replay so setup does not fail on replayed step/progress counters.

## Behavioral proof
### Live local setup / gateway wizard pass
I ran the branch locally through the real setup UI and gateway wizard. This machine's WSL online Ubuntu install path was returning `TRUST_E_BAD_DIGEST`, so for this proof run only I used an uncommitted local-rootfs override to get WSL past distro creation; no rootfs override changes are included in this PR.

After the local gateway started, the real gateway wizard ran through the WinUI `WizardPage` and advanced to permissions.

Observed live wizard states:

```text
Gateway setup -> OpenClaw setup
Gateway setup -> Config handling
Gateway setup -> QuickStart
Gateway setup -> Model/auth provider
Gateway setup -> Default model
Gateway setup -> Model check
Gateway setup -> How channels work
Gateway setup -> Select channel (QuickStart)
Gateway setup -> Web search
Gateway setup -> Search provider
Gateway setup -> Skills status
Gateway setup -> Configure skills now? (recommended)
Gateway setup -> Hooks
Gateway setup -> Enable hooks?
Grant permissions
```

Representative live UI text captured from the running app:

```text
How channels work
Docs: https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/pairing
Live gateway output
Updated config: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
Backup: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak
Workspace OK: ~/.openclaw/workspace
Sessions OK: ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions

Web search
Docs: https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/web

Grant permissions
Notifications: Enabled
Camera: Denied — open Settings to allow
Microphone: Denied — open Settings to allow
Screen Capture: Available — uses picker per capture
```

Setup log proof from the same run:

```text
step.completed: wsl-create -> Success
step.completed: start-gateway -> Success (Gateway running)
[WS] hello-ok ... methods include wizard.start, wizard.next, wizard.cancel, wizard.status
step.completed: pair-node -> Success
step.completed: verify-e2e -> Success (Gateway running; operator finalized; settings written for tray.)
Pipeline completed successfully in 166.2s
```

Screenshots from this run were captured locally for review artifacts:

```text
openclaw-live-wizard-first-step.png
openclaw-live-wizard-after-config-handling.png
openclaw-live-wizard-after-channel-info.png
openclaw-live-wizard-after-channel-skip.png
openclaw-live-wizard-after-search-skip.png
openclaw-live-wizard-after-hooks-skip.png
openclaw-live-final-permissions.png
```

### Focused protocol harness
A local proof harness (outside the repo) exercised the new protocol handling directly:

```text
classify progress: Progress
progress continues without answer: True
classify action without options: NonInteractive
action without options continues: True
classify action with options: RequiresAnswer
url line kind: Url
url highlight: https://example.test/device
code line kind: Code
code highlight: WDJB-MJHT
auth timeout: 300000
ordinary timeout: 30000
unknown-with-options wire kind: Number
unknown-with-options wire value: 42
```

This demonstrates:
- progress/status steps advance without an answer;
- action-style steps with no inputs are non-interactive;
- action-style steps with options remain answerable;
- OAuth/manual-auth URLs and device codes are detected;
- auth prompts get the extended 300s timeout;
- unknown choice-style steps preserve typed JSON option values (number `42`), not just string keys.

## Protocol/source proof
Verified against upstream OpenClaw gateway/macOS sources via GitHub MCP:

- `packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/wizard.ts` defines wizard step types including `progress` and `action`, `options[].value` as `Unknown`, `initialValue` as `Unknown`, and optional `sensitive` / `executor`.
- `src/wizard/session.ts` exposes the runtime step union: `"note" | "select" | "text" | "confirm" | "multiselect" | "progress" | "action"`, and option values are typed as `unknown`.
- `apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/OnboardingWizard.swift` consumes the same wizard start/next model, renders `progress` and `action`, preserves selected option values via `AnyCodable`, and restarts once if a wizard session is lost.

The Windows changes line up with those protocol facts while preserving Windows-specific unattended setup behavior.

## Validation
- `./build.ps1` — all projects passed.
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.SetupEngine.Tests/OpenClaw.SetupEngine.Tests.csproj` — 328 passed.
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj` — 1163 passed.
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-build` — 2422 passed / 29 skipped on rerun. The first Shared run hit a single non-deterministic failure; this branch does not touch Shared code and the rerun passed cleanly.

## Notes
- The protocol is still forward-only; this change does not add a back-step operation.
- Progress polling is bounded by both per-step and aggregate caps to prevent hangs.
target.author: bkudiess
